title: Reorder List
problem_statement: Reorder a linked list to alternate nodes from the start and end of the list.

examples.explanation: The list is reordered to bring the last node after the first, then the second-last after the second.

constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [1, 5 * 10^4]
intuition: Split the list, reverse the second half, then merge the two halves alternately.

approaches.name: Split, reverse, merge
approaches.description: Use fast/slow pointers to find the midpoint, reverse the second half, and weave the two halves together.
approaches.why_not: Using extra arrays simplifies implementation but uses additional space.

interview_qa.q: How do you find the midpoint?
interview_qa.a: Use slow and fast pointers to split the list into two halves.
Reversing enables alternating nodes from the end without random access.
